title: Content publishing operations
audience: Solo founders, consultants, and lean marketing teams
summary: Build a repeatable weekly publishing cycle without a full editorial department.
challenge: Ideas pile up but publishing slips because drafting, reviewing, and scheduling are fragmented.
workflow: Capture weekly content ideas and prioritize by business relevance.
Generate first drafts from structured prompts.
Run review checklist for clarity, claims, and CTA alignment.
Publish and queue distribution tasks with owners.
outcomes: Higher confidence in what gets shipped
openclawRole: Runs first-pass drafting, checklist automation, and publish queue coordination.
humanReview: Final editorial decisions, factual verification, and message prioritization.

title: Lead qualification and routing
audience: Service businesses and small sales teams
summary: Respond faster to inbound leads while preserving quality and context.
challenge: Inbound leads are inconsistently triaged, causing slow response times and missed opportunities.
workflow: Collect lead submissions from forms and inbox channels.
Apply qualification criteria and confidence scoring.
Generate lead summary with recommended next action.
Route to priority queue and follow-up cadence.
openclawRole: Scores leads, drafts summaries, and maintains follow-up reminders.
humanReview: Approves high-value lead decisions and custom strategic responses.

title: Recurring market research briefs
audience: Founders, product operators, and strategy teams
summary: Turn scattered market monitoring into a reliable weekly intelligence loop.
challenge: Research is inconsistent and too noisy to guide active strategic decisions.
workflow: Monitor predefined competitor and market sources.
Normalize updates into one briefing format.
Highlight trend shifts and notable anomalies.
Propose action options and route to decision owners.
openclawRole: Collects sources, drafts weekly brief, and flags unusual movement.
humanReview: Validates strategic implications and approves action recommendations.

title: Internal knowledge retrieval
audience: Small teams with growing SOP/documentation libraries
summary: Answer repeated internal questions from approved sources without constant interruption.
challenge: Operational answers vary by person and teams lose time re-answering the same questions.
workflow: Ingest approved SOPs and policy docs.
Map incoming questions to relevant source sections.
Return answer drafts with citations.
Escalate ambiguous or policy-sensitive questions.
openclawRole: Provides first-pass cited answers and tracks unanswered patterns.
humanReview: Approves sensitive policy guidance and documentation updates.

title: Weekly reporting automation
summary: Ship stakeholder-ready weekly reports with less manual prep and fewer formatting errors.
challenge: Teams spend too much time compiling recurring reports instead of acting on insights.
workflow: Pull weekly KPI snapshots from source systems.
Normalize and compare against prior periods.
Draft summary narrative and anomaly notes.
Publish reviewed report to stakeholders.
openclawRole: Aggregates metrics, drafts summaries, and formats standardized report output.
humanReview: Validates anomalies and final recommendations before distribution.

title: Customer FAQ handling
audience: Support-lean teams handling repetitive inbound questions
summary: Use a content-grounded assistant to handle repetitive FAQ traffic with clear escalation rules.
challenge: Support answers are slow or inconsistent when common questions are handled manually each time.
workflow: Build a verified FAQ/source knowledge set.
Answer common inbound questions from approved content.
Route unclear or sensitive requests to human support.
Track recurring unresolved questions to improve docs.
openclawRole: Handles first-pass FAQ responses and surfaces source-backed answers.
humanReview: Reviews escalations and updates policy-sensitive FAQ content.
